title: focus-visible standardization in ICON_BUTTON and three shadcn ui/ files, STAGE_COLORS dark variants for Applied/Phone Screen/Offer/DEFAULT
description: Continuation of the systematic UI improvement loop. This batch upgrades the ICON_BUTTON design token in designTokens.js from focus:ring-* to focus-visible:ring-*, applies the same fix to three shadcn-generated ui/ files (dialog.jsx DialogPrimitive.Close, select.jsx SelectPrimitive.Trigger, badge.jsx badgeVariants base), and adds dark: variants to the remaining four STAGE_COLORS entries in lib/constants.js (Applied, Phone Screen, Offer, and DEFAULT_STAGE_COLOR).

userStories.title: Fix designTokens.js ICON_BUTTON: focus:ring-* → focus-visible:ring-*
userStories.description: As a developer, the ICON_BUTTON token in lib/designTokens.js (line 129) uses focus:ring-2 focus:ring-brand-500 focus:ring-offset-2 — causing focus rings on mouse click. Replace all three ring classes with focus-visible: prefix.
acceptanceCriteria: In ICON_BUTTON (line 129), replace focus:ring-2 with focus-visible:ring-2, focus:ring-brand-500 with focus-visible:ring-brand-500, focus:ring-offset-2 with focus-visible:ring-offset-2
Final ring classes in ICON_BUTTON: focus:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-brand-500 focus-visible:ring-offset-2
If already using focus-visible, mark passes: true with note 'already using focus-visible'
npm run build passes with no errors
userStories.notes: Read src/lib/designTokens.js. ICON_BUTTON at line 129 is a non-shadcn custom token used in icon-only buttons across the app. The full string is 'rounded-full p-1.5 text-gray-500 hover:bg-gray-100 hover:text-gray-700 transition-colors focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-brand-500 focus:ring-offset-2 dark:text-gray-400 dark:hover:bg-gray-700 dark:hover:text-gray-200'. Replace only the three focus:ring-* classes with focus-visible: — leave focus:outline-none and all other classes unchanged.

userStories.title: Fix ui/dialog.jsx DialogPrimitive.Close: focus:ring-* → focus-visible:ring-*
userStories.description: As a developer, the DialogPrimitive.Close button in src/components/ui/dialog.jsx (line 37) uses focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-ring focus:ring-offset-2 — causing focus rings on mouse click. Replace ring classes with focus-visible: prefix.
acceptanceCriteria: On the DialogPrimitive.Close element (line ~37), replace focus:ring-2 with focus-visible:ring-2, focus:ring-ring with focus-visible:ring-ring, focus:ring-offset-2 with focus-visible:ring-offset-2
Final focus classes: focus:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring focus-visible:ring-offset-2
If already using focus-visible, mark passes: true with note 'already using focus-visible'
npm run build passes with no errors
userStories.notes: Read src/components/ui/dialog.jsx. The DialogPrimitive.Close button at line 37 has className='absolute right-4 top-4 rounded-sm opacity-70 ring-offset-background transition-opacity hover:opacity-100 focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-ring focus:ring-offset-2 disabled:pointer-events-none data-[state=open]:bg-accent data-[state=open]:text-muted-foreground'. Only change the three focus:ring-* classes to focus-visible: — leave focus:outline-none, data-[state=open]:*, disabled:*, and all other classes unchanged.

userStories.title: Fix ui/select.jsx SelectPrimitive.Trigger: focus:ring-* → focus-visible:ring-*
userStories.description: As a developer, the SelectPrimitive.Trigger in src/components/ui/select.jsx (line 17) uses focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-ring focus:ring-offset-2 — causing focus rings on mouse click. Replace ring classes with focus-visible: prefix.
acceptanceCriteria: In the cn() className at line ~17, replace focus:ring-2 with focus-visible:ring-2, focus:ring-ring with focus-visible:ring-ring, focus:ring-offset-2 with focus-visible:ring-offset-2
Final focus classes: focus:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring focus-visible:ring-offset-2
If already using focus-visible, mark passes: true with note 'already using focus-visible'
npm run build passes with no errors
userStories.notes: Read src/components/ui/select.jsx. SelectPrimitive.Trigger uses cn() to compose its className — the string at line 17 contains focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-ring focus:ring-offset-2 among many other classes. Only replace the three focus:ring-* classes. Leave all other classes in the cn() call unchanged.

userStories.title: Fix ui/badge.jsx badgeVariants base: focus:ring-* → focus-visible:ring-*
userStories.description: As a developer, the badgeVariants base string in src/components/ui/badge.jsx (line 6) uses focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-ring focus:ring-offset-2. Replace ring classes with focus-visible: prefix.
acceptanceCriteria: In the cva() base string (line ~6), replace focus:ring-2 with focus-visible:ring-2, focus:ring-ring with focus-visible:ring-ring, focus:ring-offset-2 with focus-visible:ring-offset-2
Final focus classes in the base string: focus:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring focus-visible:ring-offset-2
If already using focus-visible, mark passes: true with note 'already using focus-visible'
npm run build passes with no errors
userStories.notes: Read src/components/ui/badge.jsx. The cva() base class string at line 6 is 'inline-flex items-center rounded-full border px-2.5 py-0.5 text-xs font-semibold transition-colors focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-ring focus:ring-offset-2'. Only change the three focus:ring-* classes to focus-visible: — leave all other classes and all variant definitions unchanged.

userStories.title: Add dark: variants to STAGE_COLORS Applied in lib/constants.js
userStories.description: As a developer, lib/constants.js STAGE_COLORS Applied uses hardcoded brand color classes with no dark: variants, causing the applied stage pill to look washed out in dark mode.
acceptanceCriteria: In STAGE_COLORS.Applied (line ~4), update each property to add dark: variants:
Leave Phone Screen, Onsite, Offer, Rejected, and DEFAULT_STAGE_COLOR unchanged
If dark: variants already present on Applied, mark passes: true with note 'already has dark variants'
npm run build passes with no errors
userStories.notes: Read src/lib/constants.js. STAGE_COLORS.Applied is at line 4 with bg-brand-100, text-brand-800, bg-brand-500, border-brand-500. brand-900 is defined in tailwind.config.js as #5c3020 (confirmed). Use dark:bg-brand-900/30 for bg, dark:text-brand-300 for text, dark:bg-brand-400 for dot/activeBg, dark:border-brand-400 for border. Only touch the Applied entry — Onsite and Rejected already have dark variants from prd_56.

userStories.title: Add dark: variants to STAGE_COLORS Phone Screen in lib/constants.js
userStories.description: As a developer, lib/constants.js STAGE_COLORS 'Phone Screen' uses bg-blue-100 and text-blue-800 with no dark: variants. The dot/border/activeBg use accent-blue (#6a9bcc, a static color not a CSS variable) which also needs dark mode coverage.
acceptanceCriteria: In STAGE_COLORS['Phone Screen'] (line ~5), update each property to add dark: variants:
Leave Applied, Onsite, Offer, Rejected, and DEFAULT_STAGE_COLOR unchanged
If dark: variants already present on Phone Screen, mark passes: true with note 'already has dark variants'
npm run build passes with no errors
userStories.notes: Read src/lib/constants.js. STAGE_COLORS 'Phone Screen' is at line 5 with bg-blue-100, text-blue-800, bg-accent-blue, border-accent-blue. accent-blue is defined in tailwind.config.js as the static hex #6a9bcc (NOT a CSS variable, so it has no automatic dark variant). Use dark:bg-blue-400 as the dark variant for dot/border/activeBg — blue-400 (#60a5fa) is close to accent-blue and appropriate for dark mode. Only touch the Phone Screen entry.

userStories.title: Add dark: variants to STAGE_COLORS Offer in lib/constants.js
userStories.description: As a developer, lib/constants.js STAGE_COLORS Offer uses hardcoded emerald color classes with no dark: variants, causing the offer stage pill to look washed out in dark mode.
acceptanceCriteria: In STAGE_COLORS.Offer (line ~7), update each property to add dark: variants:
Leave Applied, Phone Screen, Onsite, Rejected, and DEFAULT_STAGE_COLOR unchanged
If dark: variants already present on Offer, mark passes: true with note 'already has dark variants'
npm run build passes with no errors
userStories.notes: Read src/lib/constants.js. STAGE_COLORS.Offer is at line 7 with bg-emerald-100, text-emerald-800, bg-emerald-500, border-emerald-500. Pattern matches Onsite (bg-amber-100 dark:bg-amber-900/30) and Rejected (bg-rose-100 dark:bg-rose-900/30) from prd_56. Use dark:bg-emerald-900/30 for bg, dark:text-emerald-300 for text, dark:bg-emerald-400 for dot/activeBg, dark:border-emerald-400 for border. Only touch the Offer entry.

userStories.title: Add dark: variants to DEFAULT_STAGE_COLOR in lib/constants.js
userStories.description: As a developer, lib/constants.js DEFAULT_STAGE_COLOR uses hardcoded gray color classes with no dark: variants, causing fallback stage pills to look washed out in dark mode.
acceptanceCriteria: In DEFAULT_STAGE_COLOR (line ~11), update each property to add dark: variants:
If dark: variants already present on DEFAULT_STAGE_COLOR, mark passes: true with note 'already has dark variants'
npm run build passes with no errors
userStories.notes: Read src/lib/constants.js. DEFAULT_STAGE_COLOR is at line 11 with bg-gray-100, text-gray-800, bg-gray-500, border-gray-400. Note that gray-100 in this project's custom palette is #f5f5f4 (very light), and gray-800 is #1f1b17 (very dark) — both problematic in dark mode. Use dark:bg-gray-800 for bg (maps to #1f1b17 — deep dark), dark:text-gray-200 for text, dark:bg-gray-400 for dot/activeBg, dark:border-gray-600 for border. Only touch DEFAULT_STAGE_COLOR, not STAGE_COLORS entries.
